response.justification: The role's most critical hard requirement is 8 years of C++ programming experience, and Bogdan's resume shows approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis side project — C++ doesn't even appear in his skills section. The remainder of his 15+ year career is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile development. While he shows genuine distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby and some AWS CDK usage at Optilyz, there is no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, no formal technical leadership tenure, and no infrastructure/hardware architecture depth at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Real distributed-systems exposure: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby, demonstrating low-latency and high-throughput engineering.
Broad technical range and polyglot background across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps tooling, suggesting adaptability.
Active knowledge-sharing culture: organized Refactoring Dojos, delivered conference talks, and ran multi-day workshops — signals mentoring aptitude.
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: fewer than 12 months documented versus the 8-year minimum requirement; C++ is absent from his listed skills.
No cloud compute platform or infrastructure-at-scale ownership — AWS CDK usage in a pricing microservice is far below the bar for 'large-scale infrastructure and distributed systems' leadership.
No verifiable technical lead or staff-engineer leadership tenure with explicit team ownership, architectural decision authority, or cross-team alignment responsibilities.
